title: Generic 2D Dirac Points Require Symmetry Protection
summary: Unlike isolated two-band crossings in three dimensions, two-dimensional Dirac points are generally not stable without extra symmetry or special structure.
assumptions: No extra protecting symmetry is built in unless stated.
scope: Lecture One contrast between 2D and 3D node stability.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One explains why 2D crossings are not generically protected in the same way.
Later examples emphasize the role of lattice symmetries in keeping Dirac points gapless.

representation: Dimension-dependent genericity claim about linear band crossings.
failure_modes: The claim changes once protective symmetries are imposed.
retrieval_hints: Use for queries contrasting graphene-like Dirac points with Weyl nodes.
